steps.title: Interpret the phrase
steps.content: The phrase no surer road means there was no more reliable way for young playwrights to see their dramas produced than going to Don Marcelo. It presents his home and support as the key path to production.

steps.title: Match the purpose
steps.content: Choice A contradicts the description of Don Marcelo receiving writers with open arms. Choice B invents a reputation contrast. Choice D concerns his later criticism, not the underlined phrase. The phrase emphasizes how consequential his support was for inexperienced playwrights. The answer is C.
